title: Assemblies and methods of making a shim
abstract: Various embodiments disclosed relate to a shim pattern. The shim pattern includes a release film. The shim pattern further includes a curable composition disposed on the release film. There are various advantages to using the shim pattern disclosed herein. For example, according to various embodiments, the shim pattern can be used to easily produce a model of a gap between two components of an aircraft (e.g., an airplane, helicopter, drone, or spacecraft) that can be used to form a shim to close the gap.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A shim pattern comprising: a release film; and a curable composition disposed on the release film. 2. The shim pattern of claim 1, wherein the release film is a first release film and the shim pattern optionally further comprises a second release film, having the curable composition disposed thereon. 3. The shim pattern of claim 2, wherein the curable composition is located between the first release film and the second release film. 4. The shim pattern of any one of claims 2 or 3, wherein the release film comprises a polyolefin, a silicon, a polyester, or mixtures thereof. 5. The shim pattern of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the release film is at least partially transparent to actinic radiation. 6. The shim pattern of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the curable composition comprises a composition that is thermally-curable, radiation-curable, chemically-curable, or a combination thereof. 7. The shim pattern of any one of claims 1 -6, wherein the curable composition is radiation-curable . 8. The shim pattern of claim 7, wherein the curable composition is at least partially cured upon exposure to actinic radiation. 9. The shim pattern of claim 7, wherein the curable composition is at least partially cured upon exposure to blue light. 10. The shim pattern of claim 9, wherein the blue light has a frequency in a range of from about 300 nm to about 500 nm. 11. The shim pattern of any one of claims 9-10, wherein the blue light has a frequency in a range of from about 450 nm to about 495 nm. 12. The shim pattern of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the curable composition comprises: a polythiol; 

 an unsaturated compound having at least two non-aromatic carbon-carbon double bonds, at least one carbon-carbon triple bond, or a combination thereof. 13. The shim pattern of claim 12, wherein the curable composition further comprises: an organoborane-amine complex; an organic peroxide; and a photoinitiator system. 14. The shim pattern of any one of claims 12 or 13, wherein the polythiol comprises an aliphatic polythiol. 15. The shim pattern of any one of claims 12-14, wherein the unsaturated compound comprises a compound having at least two vinyl groups. 16. The shim pattern of any one of claims 12-15, wherein the photoinitiator system comprises an acylphosphine-oxide . 17. The shim pattern of any one of claims 12-16, wherein the curable composition is a premixed two-part curable composition. 18. The shim pattern of claim 17, wherein a first part of the two-part curable composition comprises the polythiol and a second part of the two-part curable composition comprises the unsaturated compound. 19. The shim pattern of claim 17, wherein the curable composition is frozen. 20. The shim pattern of any one of claims 9-19, wherein an intensity of the blue light is in a range of from about 400 mW/ cm 2 to about 1000 mW7 cm 2 . 21. The shim pattern of any one of claims 9-20, wherein an intensity of the blue light is in a range of from about 600 mW/ cm 2 to about 900 mW/ cm 2 . 22. The shim pattern of any one of claims 1-21, wherein a viscosity of the curable composition at a temperature of about 3,000 Pa. s to about 10,000 Pa. s. 23. The shim pattern of any one of claims 2-22, further comprising an adhesive layer at least partially coating the release film on a surface opposite the curable composition. 24. The shim patern of claim 23, wherein the adhesive layer comprises an adhesive chosen from a pressure-sensitive adhesive, a non-pressure sensitive adhesive, a light- triggered adhesive, and mixtures thereof. 25. The shim patern of claim 24, wherein the pressure-sensitive adhesive is chosen from a natural rubber-based adhesive, a synthetic rubber based adhesive, a styrene block copolymer-based adhesive, a polyvinyl ether-based adhesive, a poly(methyl acrylate)-based adhesive, a polyolefin-based adhesive, or a silicone-based adhesive, and mixtures thereof. 26. The shim patern of claim 24, wherein the light triggered-adhesive comprises a curable adhesive composition that is configured to be cured upon exposure to ultra-violet radiation. 27. The shim patern of any one of claims 23-26, wherein the adhesive is at least partially transparent to actinic radiation. 28. The shim patern of any one of claims 1-27, further comprising a plurality of microspheres located within the curable composition. 29. The shim patern of claim 28, wherein at least one of the microspheres comprises a catalyst for curing the curable composition. 30. The shim patern of any one of claims 28 or 29, a woven hollow filament fiber fabric, or combinations thereof is transparent to actinic light. 31. The shim patern of any one of claims 1-30, wherein the release film comprises a first end and an opposite second end that are joined at an interface therebetween to at least partially envelop the curable composition. 32. The shim patern of claim 31, wherein a volume of space defined internally by the release film is greater than a volume of the curable composition. 33. The shim patern of any one of claims 31 or 32, wherein the release film comprises a perforation extending through the release film. 34. The shim patern of clam 33, wherein the release film comprises a plurality of perforations spaced across the release film. 35. The shim patern of any one of claims 23-34, wherein a thickness of at least one of the release film and the adhesive layer is independently less than a thickness of the curable composition. 36. An assembly comprising: a first substrate; a second substrate; and the shim patern of any one of claims 1-35, wherein the shim patern is in contact with the first substrate and the second substrate. 37. The assembly of claim 36, wherein the first substrate and the second substrate independently comprise an aircraft component. 38. The assembly of any one of claims 36 or 37, wherein the aircraft component is independently chosen from an aircraft skin, an aircraft fastener, an aircraft window, an aircraft access panel, a fuselage protrusion, an aircraft fuel tank, an aircraft spar, an aircraft stringer, and a combination thereof. 39. The assembly of any one of claims 36-38, wherein the shim patern is located in a gap or a seam between the first substrate and the second substrate. 40. The assembly of any one of claims 36-39, wherein the shim patern is adhered to at least one of the first substrate and the second substrate. 41. A method of making the shim patern of any one claims 2-40, the method comprising contacting the curable composition with at least one of the first release film and the second release film. 42. The method of claim 41, further comprising contacting at least one of the first release film and the second release film with an adhesive layer. 43. The method of any one of claims 41 or 42, further comprising mixing components of the curable composition before contacting the curable composition with at least one of the first release film and the second release film. 44. The method of any one of claims 41-43, further comprising freezing the curable composition. 45. The method of any one of claims 41-44, further comprising brining a first end of the first release film into contact with a second end of the first release film to at least partially envelop the curable composition. 46. The method of claim 45, further comprising joining the first end of the first release film and the second end of the first release film through heat bonding, an adhesive, or a combination thereof. 47. A method of using the shim pattern of any one of claims 36-46, the method comprising: contacting the shim pattern with at least one of the first substrate and the second substrate; moving at least one of the first substrate and the second substrate relative to each other to compress the curable composition; at least partially curing the curable composition; and removing the at least partially cured composition from contact with at least one of the first substrate and the second substrate. 48. The method of claim 47, wherein a first shape of the curable composition when the shim pattern is contacted with at least one the first substrate and the second substrate is different from a shape of the at least partially cured composition. 49. The method of any one of claims 47 or 48, wherein a shape of the at least partially cured composition is generally a negative impression of a gap or seam between the first substrate and the second substrate. 50. The method of any one of claims 47-49, further comprising fully curing the at least partially cured composition to form a cured shim pattern. 51. The method of any one of claims 47-50, wherein curing the curable composition comprises contacting at least a portion of the curable composition with actinic radiation. 52. The method of any one of claims 47-51, wherein curing the curable composition comprises contacting at least a portion of the curable composition with blue electromagnetic radiation. 53. The method of any one of claims 51 or 52, wherein curing further comprises allowing the curable composition to self-cure at ambient temperatures following contact with the actinic radiation. 54. The method of any one of claims 47-53, wherein a depth of cure of the at least partially cured curable composition is in a range of from about 40% to about 100% of the thickness of the curable composition. 55. The method of any one of claims 47-54, wherein a depth of cure of the at least partially cured curable composition is in a range of from about 80% to about 100% of the thickness of the curable composition. 56. The method of any one of claims 47-55, wherein contacting the shim pattern with at least one of the first substrate and the second substrate comprises adhering the shim pattern thereto. 57. A method of making a shim, the method comprising: providing or receiving the at least partially cured composition formed according to any one of claims 50-56; rendering a digital copy of the cured shim pattern; and producing a shim based on the digital copy of the cured shim pattern. 58. The method of claim 57, wherein digitally rendering the copy of the cured shim pattern comprises: measuring the dimensions of the cured shim pattern; and creating a digital copy of the cured shim pattern. 59. The method of any one of claims 57 or 58, wherein producing the shim comprises: using a controller to process the digital copy of the cured shim pattern in a control; and using the controller to issue a command to a device to produce the shim. 60. The method of claim 59, wherein the device is an additive manufacturing device or a machining device. 61. An aircraft comprising the shim formed according to the method of any one of claims 57-60.
